note: Certificate parser targeted payloads. Cert chain is 4th element of IM4M, parsed at 0x100012000+ BEFORE signature verification at 0x100019000+.

cases.name: cert_issuer_len64_wrap
cases.description: Cert issuer Name with 8-byte DER length (0x88). 64-bit value 0x100000020 wraps to 0x20 in 32-bit → memcpy copies 32B but parser thinks 4GB remain. Targets DER_LENGTH_COUNT_UNBOUNDED at 0x100013B0C.

cases.name: cert_serial_len64_wrap
cases.description: Cert serial INTEGER with 8-byte DER length (0x88). Wraps to 8 in 32-bit but 64-bit value = 0x100000008. Serial extractor at 0x100012000+ does memcpy with truncated size. Targets DER_LENGTH_COUNT_UNBOUNDED at 0x100013B0C.

cases.name: cert_subject_len5byte
cases.description: Cert subject Name with 5-byte DER length (0x85). Value = 0x0100000020, truncated to 0x20 in 32-bit. Subject field copier uses truncated size but structure pointer advances by full amount → pointer desync. Targets 0x100013B0C.

cases.name: cert_pubkey_overlen
cases.description: SubjectPublicKeyInfo SEQUENCE claims 0x7F0 bytes but only ~80 present. Parser reads past cert boundary into adjacent data. Key extractor memcpy at 0x10001276C copies based on claimed size → BUFFER_OVERREAD.

cases.name: cert_sig_len64_wrap
cases.description: Cert signature BIT STRING with 8-byte DER length (0x88). Wraps to 0x101 in 32-bit. Signature buffer copy at 0x100019680 uses truncated size → copies 257B to fixed-size crypto buffer. Targets pre-verify signature extraction.

cases.name: cert_deep_issuer_50
cases.description: Cert with 50-level nested issuer Name. Each RDN contains nested SET→SEQUENCE→value. Triggers UNBOUNDED_RECURSION at 0x1000123D0 and core DER walker at 0x10000D584. Combined with IMG4 container depth → total recursion ~56 levels.

cases.name: cert_deep_extensions_30
cases.description: Cert v3 with 30-level nested extensions. Each extension wraps another SEQUENCE→OCTET→SEQUENCE. Triggers recursive descent at 0x1000128C4 and 0x1000131B8. Extension parser has no depth limit — stack overflow on ARM64 with ~8KB stack.

cases.name: cert_chain_10_deep
cases.description: Certificate chain with 10 certs. Chain iterator at 0x100012DA4 is self-recursive with 2 call sites. Each cert triggers full X.509 parsing → compound recursion. 10 certs × ~6 levels each = ~60 recursive calls.

cases.name: cert_deep_pubkey_40
cases.description: SubjectPublicKeyInfo with 40 nested SEQUENCE levels. Key extractor at 0x100012000+ recursively descends into key structure. Triggers UNBOUNDED_RECURSION at 0x1000133C0 and 0x100013584 in the key material parser.

cases.name: cert_huge_issuer_cn
cases.description: Cert with 512-byte issuer CN + 256-byte Org. Name field copier at 0x10001276C does memcpy_safe with x19 = DER value length (unvalidated). If target buffer is fixed-size (e.g. 256B) → heap/stack overflow.

cases.name: cert_huge_subject_cn
cases.description: Cert with 768-byte subject CN. Subject name copier converges at 0x1000127C0 with unchecked x19 size parameter. 768B exceeds any reasonable name buffer. Targets the memcpy chain at 0x10001276C → 0x1000127C0 → 0x100012860.

cases.name: cert_huge_rsa_modulus
cases.description: Cert with 1024-byte RSA modulus (8192-bit key). Key material copier at 0x1000128A0 uses x19 from DER-parsed modulus length. If crypto buffer is sized for 4096-bit → overflow. Targets deepest cert copy at 0x1000128A0.

cases.name: cert_huge_serial
cases.description: Cert with 128-byte serial number. Serial extractor copies via memcpy_safe at 0x10000D7DC with unvalidated x19. If serial stored in fixed-size var → overflow.

cases.name: cert_huge_signature
cases.description: Cert with 1500-byte signature value. Signature copier at 0x100019680 uses x20 from parsed BIT STRING length. If RSA sig buffer is 512B → massive overflow. Executed DURING cert parsing, before verification call.

cases.name: cert_ext_keyusage_overflow
cases.description: KeyUsage extension with value causing sub w13,w11,w13 underflow at 0x100014210. Result 0xFFFFFFFF used as index in strh [x12, x8, lsl #1] → OOB write at base + 0x1FFFFFFFE. OVERFLOW_TO_MEMACCESS.

cases.name: cert_ext_huge_oid
cases.description: Extension with 255-byte OID. OID parser at 0x10001408C uses parsed bytes as array index (ldr w21, [x19, x8]). Large OID values → UNCHECKED_INDEX_FROM_PARSED OOB read. Can leak memory contents through timing side-channel.

cases.name: cert_ext_length_arith_overflow
cases.description: 15 extensions with lengths ~0xEF-0xFD. Parser at 0x100014304 does add w11, w10, #0x11 on each length → byte overflow when len ≥ 0xEF. Overflowed size used for buffer allocation/copy → heap corruption.

cases.name: cert_ext_pathlen_max
cases.description: BasicConstraints with pathLenConstraint=0xFFFFFFFF. If used as loop counter or array size in chain validation → OOB/infinite loop. Parsed at 0x100014xxx extension handler before signature verification.

cases.name: cert_unknown_sig_algo
cases.description: Cert with unknown signatureAlgorithm OID. AlgorithmIdentifier parser at 0x100012000+ dispatches to crypto function based on OID. Unknown OID may index into dispatch table OOB → calls wrong function or arbitrary address.

cases.name: cert_empty_oid_algo
cases.description: signatureAlgorithm with 0-length OID. OID decoder reads 0 bytes → uninitialized comparisons. If compared with memcmp(oid, expected, 0) → always matches → dispatches to first entry (likely RSA-SHA1 or similar).

cases.name: cert_overlong_oid_component
cases.description: signatureAlgorithm OID with overlong variable-length encoding. Last component uses max 8 continuation bytes → 63-bit decoded value. OID comparator may overflow or misparse. Targets OID decoder in cert parser region.

cases.name: cert_apple_oid_fuzz
cases.description: Extension with Apple OID prefix (1.2.840.113635.100) + oversized suffix. Apple-specific extension handler may have internal dispatch based on sub-OID. Crafted suffix → OOB in Apple extension handler lookup.

cases.name: cert_raw_tbs
cases.description: Certificate where TBSCertificate is 256 raw bytes (tag 0x41 = APPLICATION 1). X.509 parser at 0x1000123D0 expects SEQUENCE tag 0x30 → if tag not checked, parser interprets raw data as DER → controlled parsing confusion.

cases.name: cert_version_255
cases.description: Certificate with version=255 (valid: 0,1,2). If version used to index into feature/extension table → OOB. Version parsed at 0x1000123D0 entry.

cases.name: cert_empty_tbs
cases.description: Certificate with empty TBSCertificate (SEQUENCE len=0). Parser expects ≥7 fields but gets 0 → reads past structure or NULL deref on required fields.

cases.name: cert_truncated_1elem
cases.description: Certificate SEQUENCE with only TBSCertificate, no signatureAlgorithm or signature. Parser expects 3 elements → accesses [1] and [2] past end of structure.

cases.name: cert_chain_set_not_seq
cases.description: Certificate chain encoded as SET (0x31) instead of SEQUENCE (0x30). IM4M parser expects SEQUENCE at index [3] → if only length-checked without tag verification, SET data parsed as certificate → field offset mismatch.

cases.name: cert_chain_neg_serial
cases.description: Two-cert chain where second cert has serial=-1. Chain iterator at 0x100012DA4 parses each cert → negative serial in INTEGER comparison may cause signed/unsigned confusion in cert matching or lookup table indexing.

cases.name: cert_rsa4096_bigexp
cases.description: Cert with RSA-4096 modulus + 64-bit exponent (0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF). Crypto init at 0x10001901C does 8 unchecked memset_zero/memcpy calls. Oversized modulus → crypto buffer overflow. Giant exponent → infinite loop or buffer overflow in modexp.

cases.name: cert_algo_mismatch_ec_rsa
cases.description: Cert with EC public key but RSA signatureAlgorithm. Crypto dispatcher based on algo OID selects RSA verifier → fed EC point data as RSA modulus → type confusion in crypto primitive at 0x100019000+. Key size mismatch causes buffer miscalculation.

cases.name: cert_ec_point_infinity
cases.description: Cert with EC public key = point at infinity (0,0). ECDSA verifier at 0x100019000+ may divide by zero or skip point validation → any signature validates. Classic EC implementation bug — enables signature bypass.

cases.name: cert_empty_signature
cases.description: Cert with 0-byte signature (BitString with only unused-bits byte). Signature copier at 0x100019680 copies 0 bytes → crypto verifier operates on uninitialized buffer → if buffer contains previous valid sig data → bypass.

cases.name: cert_issuer_cn_0x100
cases.description: Cert with issuer CN exactly 256 bytes (0x100). WIDTH_TRUNCATION_CMP at 0x10000D530 compares w20 (32-bit) with #0x100, but x20 set by 64-bit add. If upper 32 bits nonzero → comparison gives wrong result → bypasses size validation.

cases.name: cert_len_boundary_0x7f
cases.description: Cert with issuer using length=0x7F (max short-form DER). At boundary between short (0x00-0x7F) and long form (0x81+). Parser at 0x10000D514 may select wrong branch → interprets 0x7F as long-form indicator → reads next byte as count.

cases.name: cert_indef_length_ber
cases.description: Cert issuer with indefinite length (0x80 = BER mode). DER forbids indefinite length but parser may not reject it → scans for 0x00 0x00 terminator, reading past buffer if terminator not found. Targets DER length decoder.

cases.name: cert_combo_deep_and_big
cases.description: Cert with 20-level nested issuer + 200B names + 128B RSA key. Combines recursion attack (0x1000123D0) with memcpy overflow (0x10001276C). Stack frames from recursion reduce available stack space → smaller overflow needed for corruption.

cases.name: cert_chain_progressive_depth
cases.description: 3-cert chain with progressively deeper extensions (5/10/15 levels). Chain iterator at 0x100012DA4 processes each cert → stack usage accumulates across chain. Third cert's 15-level extensions hit stack limit reduced by first two certs.

cases.name: cert_ext_len64_wrap
cases.description: Extension SEQUENCE with 8-byte DER length wrapping to 0x40. Extension parser at 0x100014304 uses DER length for buffer ops → 64-bit→32-bit truncation. Combined with UNCHECKED_INDEX at same function → controlled corruption.

cases.name: cert_plus_big_manifest_sig
cases.description: Valid-structure cert + 512B manifest signature. Manifest sig copier at 0x100017000+ copies sig to buffer before verification. If buffer < 512B → overflow. Cert parsing + manifest sig copy = two attack surfaces in one payload.

cases.name: cert_timing_minimal
cases.description: Minimal cert structure (baseline). Measures time for cert parser entry + immediate rejection. Compare with larger certs to measure parsing depth by timing delta.

cases.name: cert_timing_full_rsa
cases.description: Full X.509 cert with realistic RSA-2048 structure. Measures time including full cert field extraction + key parsing. Timing difference from minimal = cert parse time.

cases.name: cert_timing_full_ecdsa
cases.description: Full X.509 cert with ECDSA-P256 structure. Timing difference from RSA cert = dispatch path difference. If ECDSA takes longer → crypto function reached.

cases.name: cert_timing_with_extensions
cases.description: Full RSA cert + 3 standard extensions (BC, KU, SKI). Timing delta from Q1 = extension parsing overhead. Confirms whether extension parser (0x10001408C+) is reached during pre-verify cert processing.
